1	package bead
2	
3	// Backend defines the storage interface for beads.
4	// The JSONL backend is the default. The bd and br backends shell out
5	// to external tools with the same bead-compatible interface.
6	type Backend interface {
7		Init() error
8		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
9		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
10		WithLock(fn func() error) error
11	}
12	
13	// BackendType constants
14	const (
15		BackendJSONL = "jsonl"
16		BackendBD    = "bd"
17		BackendBR    = "br"
18	)
19	
20	const DefaultCollection = "beads"
21

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"path/filepath"
5		"sort"
6		"sync"
7	)
8	
9	// CollectionID is the logical name of a bead-backed collection.
10	// "beads" is the active queue; future collections (e.g. "beads-archive",
11	// "exec-runs", "agent-sessions") share the same on-disk format and store
12	// API but live under their own files and locks.
13	type CollectionID string
14	
15	// CollectionSpec is the shipping description of one collection. It maps a
16	// logical name to the concrete on-disk artifacts the JSONL backend uses.
17	//
18	// Per TD-027 §(a), the registry is an in-process table seeded at startup;
19	// it is not user-editable and DDx does not auto-discover collections from
20	// disk. Additional fields from TD-027 (QueueSemantics, ArchivePartner,
21	// Attachments) are deliberately omitted at this stage — TD-027 C3 will
22	// introduce them when archive logic lands. This bead (C2) is scoped to the
23	// storage-engine refactor: registry shape plus path/lock decoupling.
24	type CollectionSpec struct {
25		// ID is the logical collection name, e.g. "beads".
26		ID CollectionID
27		// JSONLFile is the file name (not a full path) under the .ddx
28		// directory that the JSONL backend reads and writes. Empty means
29		// "<id>.jsonl".
30		JSONLFile string
31		// LockDirName is the lock directory name under the .ddx directory.
32		// Empty means "<id>.lock".
33		LockDirName string
34	}
35	
36	// FileName returns the JSONL filename for this collection, falling back to
37	// the conventional "<id>.jsonl" form when JSONLFile is unset.
38	func (c CollectionSpec) FileName() string {
39		if c.JSONLFile != "" {
40			return c.JSONLFile
41		}
42		return string(c.ID) + ".jsonl"
43	}
44	
45	// LockName returns the lock-directory name for this collection, falling
46	// back to the conventional "<id>.lock" form when LockDirName is unset.
47	func (c CollectionSpec) LockName() string {
48		if c.LockDirName != "" {
49			return c.LockDirName
50		}
51		return string(c.ID) + ".lock"
52	}
53	
54	// PathsUnder returns the absolute file and lock paths for this collection
55	// rooted at the given .ddx directory.
56	func (c CollectionSpec) PathsUnder(ddxDir string) (file, lockDir string) {
57		return filepath.Join(ddxDir, c.FileName()), filepath.Join(ddxDir, c.LockName())
58	}
59	
60	// Registry is a table of known collection specs keyed by ID. Lookups for
61	// IDs that are not registered are not an error: NewStore synthesizes a
62	// conventional spec on the fly so existing call sites that use ad-hoc
63	// collection names (e.g. test fixtures) keep working. The registry is the
64	// authoritative source for the shipping collections that DDx itself
65	// instantiates.
66	type Registry struct {
67		mu    sync.RWMutex
68		specs map[CollectionID]CollectionSpec
69	}
70	
71	// NewRegistry returns an empty registry.
72	func NewRegistry() *Registry {
73		return &Registry{specs: make(map[CollectionID]CollectionSpec)}
74	}
75	
76	// Register adds or replaces a spec.
77	func (r *Registry) Register(spec CollectionSpec) {
78		r.mu.Lock()
79		defer r.mu.Unlock()
80		r.specs[spec.ID] = spec
81	}
82	
83	// Lookup returns the spec for id and whether it was registered. Callers
84	// that want a usable spec for any name should prefer Resolve.
85	func (r *Registry) Lookup(id CollectionID) (CollectionSpec, bool) {
86		r.mu.RLock()
87		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
88		spec, ok := r.specs[id]
89		return spec, ok
90	}
91	
92	// Resolve returns the spec for id, synthesizing a conventional one (with
93	// "<id>.jsonl" / "<id>.lock") when id is not registered. This keeps the
94	// JSONL backend's per-collection layout uniform whether or not a name is
95	// in the shipping table.
96	func (r *Registry) Resolve(id CollectionID) CollectionSpec {
97		if spec, ok := r.Lookup(id); ok {
98			return spec
99		}
100		return CollectionSpec{ID: id}
101	}
102	
103	// IDs returns the registered collection IDs in lexical order.
104	func (r *Registry) IDs() []CollectionID {
105		r.mu.RLock()
106		defer r.mu.RUnlock()
107		out := make([]CollectionID, 0, len(r.specs))
108		for id := range r.specs {
109			out = append(out, id)
110		}
111		sort.Slice(out, func(i, j int) bool { return out[i] < out[j] })
112		return out
113	}
114	
115	// defaultRegistry is the process-wide registry. It ships with the active
116	// "beads" collection and its archive partner "beads-archive" (TD-027 §a).
117	// Additional collections (exec-runs, agent-sessions) will be registered by
118	// their owning beads.
119	var defaultRegistry = func() *Registry {
120		r := NewRegistry()
121		r.Register(CollectionSpec{
122			ID:          DefaultCollection,
123			JSONLFile:   DefaultCollection + ".jsonl",
124			LockDirName: DefaultCollection + ".lock",
125		})
126		r.Register(CollectionSpec{
127			ID:          BeadsArchiveCollection,
128			JSONLFile:   BeadsArchiveCollection + ".jsonl",
129			LockDirName: BeadsArchiveCollection + ".lock",
130		})
131		return r
132	}()
133	
134	// DefaultRegistry returns the process-wide collection registry.
135	func DefaultRegistry() *Registry {
136		return defaultRegistry
137	}
138

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
8		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
9	)
10	
11	// TestBdSchemaCompatibility verifies that DDx bead JSONL output matches the
12	// bd (Dolt-backed bead tracker) schema exactly. This test locks in the field
13	// names so we never accidentally diverge from bd/br interchange format.
14	func TestBdSchemaCompatibility(t *testing.T) {
15		// This is a real bd export line (from `bd export --format jsonl`)
16		bdExport := `{"id":"bd-test-1x4","title":"test issue","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"user@example.com","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:23:39Z","created_by":"Test User","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:23:39Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-test-1x4","depends_on_id":"bd-test-ioz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T21:23:54Z","created_by":"Test User","metadata":"{}"}],"dependency_count":1,"dependent_count":0,"comment_count":0}`
17	
18		// DDx must be able to unmarshal bd export without loss of known fields
19		b, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(bdExport))
20		require.NoError(t, err)
21	
22		assert.Equal(t, "bd-test-1x4", b.ID)
23		assert.Equal(t, "test issue", b.Title)
24		assert.Equal(t, "open", b.Status)
25		assert.Equal(t, 2, b.Priority)
26		assert.Equal(t, "task", b.IssueType)
27		assert.Equal(t, "user@example.com", b.Owner)
28		assert.Equal(t, "Test User", b.CreatedBy)
29		assert.False(t, b.CreatedAt.IsZero())
30		assert.False(t, b.UpdatedAt.IsZero())
31	
32		// Dependencies must be parsed
33		require.Len(t, b.Dependencies, 1)
34		assert.Equal(t, "bd-test-1x4", b.Dependencies[0].IssueID)
35		assert.Equal(t, "bd-test-ioz", b.Dependencies[0].DependsOnID)
36		assert.Equal(t, "blocks", b.Dependencies[0].Type)
37	
38		// bd-specific computed fields (dependency_count etc.) should be preserved in Extra
39		assert.Equal(t, float64(1), b.Extra["dependency_count"])
40		assert.Equal(t, float64(0), b.Extra["dependent_count"])
41		assert.Equal(t, float64(0), b.Extra["comment_count"])
42	}
43	
44	// TestBdRoundTrip verifies that a bd-exported bead survives DDx unmarshal→marshal
45	// without losing or renaming any fields.
46	func TestBdRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
47		bdExport := `{"id":"bd-test-abc","title":"Round trip","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"dev@co.com","created_at":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":"Dev","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:00:00Z","custom_field":"preserved","dependency_count":0}`
48	
49		b, err := unmarshalBead([]byte(bdExport))
50		require.NoError(t, err)
51	
52		// Marshal back
53		out, err := marshalBead(b)
54		require.NoError(t, err)
55	
56		// Parse the output and verify all original fields survive
57		var result map[string]any
58		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(out, &result))
59	
60		// bd-compatible field names
61		assert.Equal(t, "bd-test-abc", result["id"])
62		assert.Equal(t, "Round trip", result["title"])
63		assert.Equal(t, "open", result["status"])
64		assert.Equal(t, float64(1), result["priority"])
65		assert.Equal(t, "epic", result["issue_type"])
66		assert.Equal(t, "dev@co.com", result["owner"])
67		assert.Equal(t, "Dev", result["created_by"])
68	
69		// Custom fields preserved via Extra
70		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", result["custom_field"])
71	
72		// Verify NO old DDx field names leak through
73		assert.Nil(t, result["type"], "must use issue_type, not type")
74		assert.Nil(t, result["assignee"], "must use owner, not assignee")
75		assert.Nil(t, result["created"], "must use created_at, not created")
76		assert.Nil(t, result["updated"], "must use updated_at, not updated")
77		assert.Nil(t, result["deps"], "must use dependencies, not deps")
78	}
79	
80	// TestDdxBeadFieldNames verifies that DDx-created beads use bd-compatible
81	// field names in their JSONL output.
82	func TestDdxBeadFieldNames(t *testing.T) {
83		s := newTestStore(t)
84	
85		b := &Bead{Title: "New bead", IssueType: "task"}
86		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
87	
88		// Marshal and check field names
89		out, err := marshalBead(*b)
90		require.NoError(t, err)
91	
92		var result map[string]any
93		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal(out, &result))
94	
95		// Must use bd-compatible names
96		assert.Contains(t, result, "issue_type", "must use issue_type")
97		assert.Contains(t, result, "created_at", "must use created_at")
98		assert.Contains(t, result, "updated_at", "must use updated_at")
99	
100		// Must NOT use old DDx names
101		assert.NotContains(t, result, "type", "must not use 'type' (use issue_type)")
102		assert.NotContains(t, result, "assignee", "must not use 'assignee' (use owner)")
103		assert.NotContains(t, result, "created", "must not use 'created' (use created_at)")
104		assert.NotContains(t, result, "updated", "must not use 'updated' (use updated_at)")
105		assert.NotContains(t, result, "deps", "must not use 'deps' (use dependencies)")
106	}

68	func TestExternalBackendCarriesLogicalCollectionName(t *testing.T) {
69		toolDir := t.TempDir()
70		writeFakeBackendTool(t, toolDir, "bd")
71		writeFakeBackendTool(t, toolDir, "br")
72		t.Setenv("PATH", toolDir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
73	
74		for _, tc := range []struct {
75			name       string
76			backend    string
77			collection string
78		}{
79			{name: "default-bd", backend: "bd", collection: DefaultCollection},
80			{name: "exec-runs-bd", backend: "bd", collection: "exec-runs"},
81			{name: "agent-sessions-br", backend: "br", collection: "agent-sessions"},
82		} {
83			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
84				t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_BACKEND", tc.backend)
85				s := NewStore(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".ddx"), WithCollection(tc.collection))
86				backend, ok := s.backend.(*ExternalBackend)
87				require.True(t, ok)
88				assert.Equal(t, tc.backend, backend.Tool)
89				assert.Equal(t, tc.collection, backend.Collection)
90			})
91		}
92	}

18	// TestMain scrubs all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
19	// test subprocesses don't inherit lefthook's GIT_DIR/GIT_WORK_TREE and leak
20	// into the parent repo's config.
21	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
22		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
23			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
24				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
25					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
26				}
27			}
28		}
29		os.Exit(m.Run())
30	}

1	//go:build !windows
2	
3	package bead
4	
5	import "syscall"
6	
7	// processAlive checks if a process with the given PID exists.
8	// Uses signal 0 which checks existence without sending a signal.
9	func processAlive(pid int) bool {
10		err := syscall.Kill(pid, 0)
11		// ESRCH = no such process (dead). EPERM = exists but different user (alive). nil = alive.
12		return err != syscall.ESRCH
13	}

feat(bead): external backend JSONL fallback for non-default collections [ddx-9f7a04f4]

bd and br lack per-collection scoping in their CLI surface. To let
ExternalBackend serve collections like beads-archive without breaking the
bd/br interchange contract for the default beads collection, route
non-default collections through a JSONL fallback under .ddx/<collection>.jsonl.

Default collection stays unchanged: schema_compat_test.go and the
existing import/export round-trip continue to flow through bd/br.
EOF
)"

data.text:
Committed. Summary of work:

- **`backend_external.go`**: ExternalBackend now carries an optional `fallback Backend`. New constructor `NewExternalBackendWithFallback` wires the fallback for non-default collections; default collection still routes straight to bd/br to preserve the interchange contract.
- **`backend_jsonl.go`** (new): `JSONLBackend` implements the Backend interface using the existing `parseBeadJSONL`/`marshalBead`/`writeAtomicFile`/`acquireDirLock` helpers — used as the fallback under `.ddx/<collection>.jsonl`.
- **`lock.go`**: Extracted `acquireDirLock` / `breakStaleLockDir` as free functions so JSONLBackend reuses Store's lock primitive.
- **`store.go`**: NewStore wires the JSONL fallback when collection != default; routing decision documented inline.
- **`store_test.go`**: Added `TestExternalBackendOpensBeadsArchiveWithFallback` (proves bd is *never* invoked for `beads-archive`, full Init/Read/Write round-trip succeeds via fallback) and `TestExternalBackendDefaultCollectionHasNoFallback` (locks in default-collection contract).

`schema_compat_test.go`, existing external-backend tests, and the broader bead suite all pass.
